groups.name: spring.gdpr
groups.description: spring-gdpr starter: GDPR compliance-by-annotation. Audit log, retention enforcement, right-to-erasure, audit-access REST.

groups.name: spring.gdpr.audit
groups.description: Audit-log persistence. Default sink is SLF4J. Enable JDBC for the Art. 15 right-of-access endpoint.

groups.name: spring.gdpr.audit.async
groups.description: Async-decorator config. ON by default. Drop-newest under saturation, observable via dropped_total counter.

groups.name: spring.gdpr.retention
groups.description: Retention enforcement (Art. 5(1)(e)). Cron-driven sweep over registered RetentionTarget beans.

groups.name: spring.gdpr.erasure
groups.description: Right-to-erasure flow (Art. 17). Delegates to registered ErasureHandler beans in declared order.

groups.name: spring.gdpr.web
groups.description: REST surface (/gdpr/erasure, /gdpr/audit/access). Wire Spring Security around the configured base-path.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.enabled
properties.description: Master switch. When false, no advisor, scheduler, or REST endpoint is registered.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.jdbc-enabled
properties.description: Persist audit records to a JDBC table (instead of SLF4J only). Required for the Art. 15 right-of-access endpoint.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.table
properties.description: Database table for audit log. Must match the migration script (default 'gdpr_audit_access'). Whitelist-validated, only [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* accepted.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.auto-create-schema
properties.description: Dev-only shortcut. When true, JdbcAuditSink issues CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on first use. Production deployments must apply the bundled migration via Flyway/Liquibase instead.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.async.enabled
properties.description: Wrap the audit sink with AsyncAuditSinkDecorator. Production deployments should never block the request thread on audit I/O.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.async.thread-count
properties.description: Worker thread count. Default 1 fits most log-aggregation and indexed JDBC workloads.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.async.queue-capacity
properties.description: Bounded queue capacity. Drop-newest under saturation, observable via dropped_total counter and WARN logs.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.audit.async.await-millis
properties.description: Milliseconds to wait for the executor to drain on shutdown before forcing termination.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.retention.enabled
properties.description: Whether the retention scheduler runs. Disable to defer enforcement to a batch job.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.retention.cron
properties.description: Cron expression for the retention sweep. Default: every day at 03:00.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.erasure.rest-enabled
properties.description: Whether the DELETE /gdpr/erasure/{subjectId} REST endpoint is exposed.

properties.name: spring.gdpr.web.base-path
properties.description: Base path for spring-gdpr REST endpoints. Wire Spring Security rules around <base-path>/**.

values.description: Default table name, matches the bundled Flyway migration V1__gdpr_audit_access.sql.
Every 6 hours. Use for high-volume retention with short cutoffs.
